This is censorship and we should not tolerate this as long as the material (here a game) sold is completely legal.

paragraphs.text: The Digital Fairness Act is a great move in the right direction. The gaming community would also benefit from it if it would also tackle the ownership of games since nowadays they mostly get 'killed' and players around the world lose access to the game they bought with their hard earned money, 60 to 90(!) for one game that can just disappear (looking at Overwatch 1 for instance) isn't the greatest feeling. We could also prevent any payment processors from having their say in what a store is selling. We recently had the case on itch(dot)io and Steam where games were removed because of payment processors. This is censorship and we should not tolerate this as long as the material (here a game) sold is completely legal. Thank you for reading.
